reasoning: The Lean statement introduces μ, Var, M as abstract ℝ-valued functions characterized by hypotheses that match the informal definitions exactly: (1) `hμ` gives μ f as ∫ x, f x, where the default MeasureSpace on ↥(Icc 0 1) — registered in Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Constructions/UnitInterval.lean as Measure.Subtype.measureSpace, which is a probability measure — yields ∫₀¹ f(x) dx; (2) `hVar` gives Var f = ∫ x, (f x - μ f)² as required; (3) `hM` uses IsGreatest on range (abs ∘ f), which on ℝ uniquely determines M f as max_{x ∈ [0,1]} |f(x)| (the max exists since f is continuous on a compact nonempty set).
